team_context.cap_situation: tight — currently over the cap before restructures, but $24M+ in effective space with projected restructures of Stingley, Collins, Pitre. Extensions pending for Will Anderson Jr. and possibly C.J. Stroud.
team_context.trade_posture: Aggressive buyers trying to win now. Made 3+ trades and multiple extensions already this offseason. Have 8 draft picks including two 2nds. Willing to move depth pieces but retaining core at all costs.

players.name: C.J. Stroud
players.performance_evidence: 2023 OROY (4,108 yds, 23 TD, 5 INT) but has regressed each year since. In 2025: 3,041 yds, 19 TD, 8 INT in 14 games. Threw 4 INT in playoff loss to Patriots (21.1 QBR). Still just 24 and on a cheap rookie deal. Team expected to pick up 5th-year option ($26.5M for 2027).

trade_value.reasoning: Despite regression, a 24-year-old former #2 pick QB on the final year of a $11.5M cap hit is extraordinarily valuable. Massive positional premium. Has led team to 3 straight playoffs. Extension-eligible — acquiring team gets a potential franchise QB at bargain price with option year available.

players.availability_reasoning: DeMeco Ryans says 'C.J. is our guy' but Spotrac notes 'growing questions at QB1.' Rapoport expects 5th-year option to be exercised. Extension could be pushed to 2027. A shocking offer (multiple 1sts) could theoretically pry him loose, but the team's stated posture is to keep him.
players.injury_notes: Missed 3 games in 2025 (undisclosed). Has been durable otherwise.
players.trade_buzz: Rapoport noted teams could 'swoop in to see if they could pry the wildly talented 24-year-old away' if extension talks get sticky.

players.name: Davis Mills
players.performance_evidence: Capable backup who started several games in 2025 when Stroud was out. Signed a 1-year, $7M extension with Houston. 5-19-1 record as starter historically but valued as experienced insurance behind Stroud.

trade_value.reasoning: Experienced backup QB on a reasonable 1-year deal. Minimal trade value — worth a late-round pick swap at best. Houston values him as championship-window insurance.

players.availability_reasoning: If Texans draft a QB or feel confident in Stroud's health, Mills could be moved for a late pick. But team views backup QB insurance as important in their window.

players.name: David Montgomery
players.performance_evidence: Just traded from Lions for a 4th-rounder + OL + 7th. Career 4,847 rushing yards, 33 rushing TDs since 2023. Had reduced role in 2025 (158 carries — career low) but has been a consistent 1,000-yard rusher when featured. Turns 29 in June.

trade_value.reasoning: Just acquired for a 4th-round pick equivalent package. 29-year-old RB with 2 years left but zero guarantees. Position devaluation and age limit trade value. Worth a 5th-round pick at best given RB market.

players.availability_reasoning: Just acquired — team clearly wanted him. Expected to be RB1. Would only move if a surprisingly good offer came in, but no incentive to sell immediately after trading for him.
players.injury_notes: Has been durable throughout career. Fresh legs after reduced workload in 2025.

players.name: Woody Marks
players.performance_evidence: UDFA who earned RB1 role as a rookie in 2025, rushing for 703 yards on 196 carries (3.6 YPC). Showed playmaking ability but was inconsistent. Now RB2 behind Montgomery.

trade_value.reasoning: Young UDFA with a year of starting experience but limited production. On a minimum salary. Worth a conditional late-round pick at most.

players.availability_reasoning: With Montgomery now in the fold, Marks is depth. Could be moved if a team offered a late-round pick, but his cheap contract makes him easy to keep.

players.name: Nico Collins
players.performance_evidence: 2025 Pro Bowler: 71 catches, 1,117 yards, 6 TDs on 120 targets. Named to Pro Bowl in both 2024 and 2025. 6-4, 222 lbs with 4.44 speed — prototypical WR1. Missed playoff loss vs Patriots due to concussion.

trade_value.reasoning: Pro Bowl WR in his prime at 27 with 2 years remaining on a fair market deal ($24.25M AAV). Elite physical tools. Worth a late 1st-round pick. Dead cap of $20M makes a trade unlikely from Houston's side.

players.availability_reasoning: Core offensive weapon. 2 years, $43M remaining. $20M dead cap makes trade financially punitive. Collins has said he wants to be in Houston 'forever.' Texans view him as foundational alongside Stroud.
players.injury_notes: Concussion in 2025 Wild Card game caused him to miss Divisional Round. Some concussion history to monitor.

players.name: Jayden Higgins
players.performance_evidence: 2025 rookie: 41 catches, 525 yards, 6 TDs in 17 games. First second-round pick in NFL history with a fully guaranteed contract ($11.7M). 6-4 possession receiver who showed growth as the season progressed. Became reliable WR2 behind Collins.

trade_value.reasoning: Young WR on a cheap fully-guaranteed rookie deal with 3 years of control. Showed promise but wasn't a breakout star. Worth an early-to-mid 3rd based on rookie production, age, and contract.

players.availability_reasoning: 2025 2nd-round pick who is part of the long-term plan. Cheap contract through 2028. Would only be moved in a package deal for a significant upgrade. Not being shopped.

players.name: Tank Dell
players.performance_evidence: Missed entire 2025 season recovering from torn ACL, LCL, and MCL suffered in December 2024. Was a promising WR in 2023 (709 yds, 7 TD). On schedule to return for 2026 but significant injury risk. Contract year in 2026.

trade_value.reasoning: Coming off devastating multi-ligament knee injury that cost him all of 2025. In a contract year with no guarantees. Upside exists but extreme injury risk. Worth a conditional late-round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: Spotrac notes Houston can save $1.5M by cutting him. Team drafted Higgins and Noel to replace him. Beat reporters suggest he doesn't make sense for the Texans anymore. Could be cut or traded for minimal return.
players.injury_notes: Torn ACL, LCL, and MCL in December 2024. Missed entire 2025 season. On schedule to return for 2026 but long-term durability is a major concern.
players.trade_buzz: Multiple reports suggest Texans may cut Dell outright. His roster spot is in jeopardy.

players.name: Dalton Schultz
players.performance_evidence: Led Texans with 82 receptions for 777 yards, 3 TDs in 2025. Stroud's security blanket and #1 target by volume. Just signed a 1-year, $12.6M extension through 2027 with $17.6M now guaranteed. Reliable pass-catcher, improved blocker.

trade_value.reasoning: Quality starter TE with 2 years remaining after fresh extension. Turning 30 but coming off career-best reception total. $17.6M guaranteed complicates a trade. Worth a mid-4th-round pick equivalent.

players.availability_reasoning: Just extended through 2027, clearly valued. However, $15.9M cap hit in 2026 is hefty for a non-elite TE. If Texans need cap relief, he could theoretically be moved post-June 1, but unlikely given how recently the extension was signed.
players.trade_buzz: Earlier reports mentioned Schultz as a potential trade candidate, but the extension signals commitment.

players.name: Brevin Jordan
players.performance_evidence: Depth TE behind Schultz. Limited offensive contribution — primarily a blocking TE. Has flashed receiving ability in spot duty. Texans also signed Foster Moreau this offseason.

trade_value.reasoning: Backup TE on a minimum deal. Essentially no trade value — a roster filler.

players.availability_reasoning: With Schultz extended and Moreau signed, Jordan's roster spot is not secure. Could be cut or traded for a conditional pick.

players.name: Aireontae Ersery
players.performance_evidence: 2025 2nd-round pick who started at LT as a rookie following Laremy Tunsil trade. PFF: 62.6 pass blocking grade, 47.0 run blocking grade at midseason. Allowed 7 sacks and 18 hurries. Raw but has upside at 6-6, 331 lbs. Former Big Ten OL of the Year.

trade_value.reasoning: Young OT on a cheap rookie deal with 3 years of control. Below-average rookie year but significant physical upside and draft pedigree. OT premium. Worth a late 2nd/early 3rd given age and contract.

players.availability_reasoning: Long-term LT solution following Tunsil trade. Team invested heavily to draft him. Would only move for a significant package that included a proven tackle replacement.

players.name: Jarrett Patterson
players.performance_evidence: UDFA who has worked his way into a starting role. Versatile interior lineman who can play guard and center. Solid but unspectacular — part of a reshuffled OL unit.

trade_value.reasoning: Fringe starter on a minimum deal. Useful versatility but limited upside. Worth a late-round pick swap at best.

players.availability_reasoning: With Ingram re-signed at RG, Patterson's starting spot at LG is not locked in. Could be moved if Texans add another guard in the draft.

players.name: Jake Andrews
players.performance_evidence: 2024 5th-round pick who has become the starting center. Young and developing. Part of a completely revamped interior OL. Cheap contract through 2027.

trade_value.reasoning: Young starting center on a cheap rookie deal. Limited sample size but positional need across the league gives him some value. Worth a late 5th-round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: Young, cheap, and starting. Texans need OL stability — not looking to move him unless they land a clear upgrade.

players.name: Ed Ingram
players.performance_evidence: Breakout 2025 season after trade from Vikings. PFF top-10 run blocking guard. Allowed 12th-lowest pressure rate (6.4%) among guards with 400+ pass-block snaps. Just signed 3-year, $37.5M extension. Best OL on Texans roster in 2025.

trade_value.reasoning: 27-year-old quality starter guard who just signed a new 3-year deal at a good rate ($12.5M AAV is top-20 but fair). Dead cap of ~$18M makes a trade impractical. Worth a mid-to-late 2nd based on production and contract.

players.availability_reasoning: Just re-signed specifically to anchor the OL rebuild. $23.5M guaranteed and high dead cap make moving him financially impossible. Core piece of revamped offensive line.

players.name: Braden Smith
players.performance_evidence: Just signed 2-year, $25M deal with $13.5M guaranteed. 8-year veteran with 105 starts in 107 games for Colts. In 2025: 13 starts, allowed 1 sack and 6 QB hits in 471 pass-blocking snaps per PFF. Solid technician, not elite.

trade_value.reasoning: Veteran RT on a fair 2-year deal. Turning 30 limits upside but provides stability. Just signed — $13.5M guaranteed makes an immediate trade financially painful. Worth a mid-4th based on age and production.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed to fix the OL. Unlikely to be moved in 2026. Could become available in 2027 if a young tackle develops.
players.injury_notes: Has missed time in concussion protocol. Played 35 of 51 possible games since 2023 due to physical and mental health reasons.

players.name: Danielle Hunter
players.performance_evidence: 15 sacks, 3 forced fumbles, 1 fumble recovery in 2025. Five-time Pro Bowler. Double-digit sacks in 7 of 10 NFL seasons. 12 sacks + 90 QB pressures (2nd in NFL) in 2024. Just signed 1-year, $40.1M extension (fully guaranteed) through 2026.

trade_value.reasoning: Elite EDGE production at 31, but $95M dead cap makes him literally untradeable. The massive restructured contract with void years means Houston cannot move him. If tradeable, would be worth a late 1st.

players.availability_reasoning: $95M dead cap if traded. This is not a football decision — it is a financial impossibility. Hunter is locked in Houston through 2026 by contract structure alone.
players.injury_notes: Has missed time throughout career but remarkably durable the past 2 seasons.

players.name: Will Anderson Jr.
players.performance_evidence: 12 sacks in 2025 (tied 8th in NFL), first-team All-Pro. 3 sacks and 2 forced fumbles in playoff loss to Patriots. 25.9% pressure rate (1st in NFL with Hunter). Extension expected at $40M+ per year — potentially highest-paid defender in NFL.

trade_value.reasoning: 24-year-old All-Pro EDGE on the final year of a rookie deal with 5th-year option available. This is one of the most valuable non-QB assets in the NFL. Multiple 1sts wouldn't be enough. Extension imminent.

players.availability_reasoning: Franchise cornerstone. Team will exercise 5th-year option ($27M for 2027) and is actively negotiating a massive extension projected at $40M+ annually. Rapoport and Spotrac both expect a deal done this offseason. Zero chance of trade.

players.name: Tommy Togiai
players.performance_evidence: Breakout 2025: 59 tackles (27 solo), 1.5 sacks, 4 pass defenses, fumble recovery for TD in 15 games. 71.4 PFF grade (23rd among IDL). 10-tackle game vs Chiefs in prime time. DeMeco Ryans calls him 'Mr. Consistent.'

trade_value.reasoning: Emerging DT on a cheap 1-year deal in a contract year. Good production but only one strong season. Worth a mid-to-late 4th-round pick. Expiring deal limits value.

players.availability_reasoning: Valuable rotational piece who earned a starting role. On a cheap deal. Team would need a solid offer to move him given defensive depth is important to their identity.

players.name: Sheldon Rankins
players.performance_evidence: Veteran DT who provides leadership in the defensive line room. Had his original deal void in March but re-signed on a new 2-year deal. Rotational player behind Togiai. Former 1st-round pick whose best years are behind him.

trade_value.reasoning: 32-year-old rotational DT. Veteran presence but limited on-field value in trade market. Worth a conditional 7th at best.

players.availability_reasoning: Veteran depth piece. Re-signed on a modest deal. Could be moved if team needs cap space or roster flexibility.

players.name: Henry To'oTo'o
players.performance_evidence: Started 35 of 46 career games since 2023 draft. Reliable starter alongside Pro Bowler Azeez Al-Shaair. Entering final year of rookie deal — contract year in 2026. Solid against the run, developing in coverage.

trade_value.reasoning: 25-year-old starting LB in a contract year on a minimum deal. Cheap and productive — good value. Worth a mid-to-late 4th-round pick. Expiring deal is the limiting factor.

players.availability_reasoning: Starter on the NFL's best defense. In a contract year with no guarantees. Team could let him play out the year or extend him. Would need a solid offer to trade him given LB depth concerns.

players.name: E.J. Speed
players.performance_evidence: 62 tackles, 3 TFL, 1 PD in 16 games (9 starts) in 2025. 9.6% run stop rate (5th among LBs with 150+ run snaps). Also a core special teams contributor. Just re-signed on 2-year, $13M deal with $7.5M guaranteed.

trade_value.reasoning: 30-year-old LB who serves as LB3/special teams ace. Just re-signed — guaranteed money complicates a trade. Worth a late-round pick at most.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed a new deal, so not being shopped. But at 30 and as a depth LB, if a team offered a pick Houston could consider it. Low priority to move.

players.name: Derek Stingley Jr.
players.performance_evidence: Two-time First-Team All-Pro, two-time Pro Bowler. In 2025: 4 INTs, franchise-record-tying secondary performance. In 2024: 5 INTs, 18 PD (2nd in NFL), 84.4 PFF coverage grade. Allowed 46.7% completion rate, 56.9 passer rating in 2024. Highest-paid CB in NFL history ($90M extension, $89M guaranteed).

trade_value.reasoning: 25-year-old elite CB locked up long-term. Restructured deal drops 2026 cap hit to ~$10.8M. $65M+ dead cap makes trade impossible. If hypothetically available, would command multiple 1sts.

players.availability_reasoning: Franchise cornerstone. Just restructured his $90M extension. $65M dead cap makes any trade financially catastrophic. Anchors the NFL's best secondary.
players.injury_notes: Early career injury concerns (missed 14 games in first 2 seasons) but fully healthy last 2 years with 33 straight starts.

players.name: Kamari Lassiter
players.performance_evidence: 2025: 91 tackles, 17 PD, 4 INTs in 16 games. Named Pro Bowl alternate in both 2024 and 2025 (added to 2026 Pro Bowl Games). 77.5 PFF coverage grade (11th among CBs with 500+ pass snaps) as a rookie. Started every game. Called 'The Locksmith' by teammates.

trade_value.reasoning: 23-year-old Pro Bowl-caliber CB2 on a cheap rookie deal with 2 years remaining. Elite young talent at a premium position. Would command a 1st-round pick and then some. One of the most valuable young defensive assets in the NFL.

players.availability_reasoning: Core part of the NFL's best secondary. 23 years old on a minimum rookie deal. Extension decision looming in 2025-2026. Zero chance Houston trades him.
players.injury_notes: Missed Week 18 and was limited in playoffs with ankle/knee issues. Nothing long-term.

players.name: Jalen Pitre
players.performance_evidence: Pro Bowl alternate in 2025. Key chess piece as nickel/safety hybrid in Texans' #1 defense. 3-year, $39M extension ($29.15M guaranteed) signed April 2025. Just restructured to clear $5.1M in 2026 cap space. Hard-hitting versatile defender.

trade_value.reasoning: 26-year-old versatile DB on a 3-year deal with restructured cap hit of only $5M in 2026. Pro Bowl-caliber nickel is rare and valuable. Dead cap of $16M makes trade highly unlikely. Worth a mid-to-late 1st if available.

players.availability_reasoning: Just extended and restructured. $16M dead cap. Core defensive piece. DeMeco Ryans' scheme revolves around his versatility. No chance of trade.
players.injury_notes: Shoulder injury ended 2024 season in Week 12. Returned healthy for 2025.

players.name: Reed Blankenship
players.performance_evidence: Just signed 3-year, $24.75M deal ($16.5M guaranteed) from Eagles. Super Bowl champion. Started 50 games over past 3 seasons. 308 career tackles, 9 INTs (4 in 2024). Dependable, high-floor safety who communicates well.

trade_value.reasoning: 27-year-old solid starting safety on a fair 3-year deal. Just signed — $16.5M guaranteed makes an immediate trade impossible. Worth a mid-4th if hypothetically available.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed as a free agent. Team specifically targeted him. Would not trade him in 2026 under any realistic scenario given guaranteed money.

players.name: Jaylen Reed
players.performance_evidence: 6th-round rookie who earned a starting role briefly in 2025. Part of the safety rotation. Showed enough to be listed as FS on the depth chart heading into 2026. Very cheap on a rookie deal.

trade_value.reasoning: Late-round rookie safety with limited starting experience. On a minimum deal. Essentially no trade value — a developmental piece.

players.availability_reasoning: Depth safety who could be displaced by Blankenship's signing. Available if a team offered anything of value, but also cheap enough to keep.

players.name: Evan Brown
players.performance_evidence: Brown started at center for the Lions in 2022-2023 and was a key part of one of the NFL's best offensive lines. Signed with Houston as a free agent and transitioned to guard, where he has been a solid, versatile presence. PFF grades have consistently been in the 65-72 range at guard — not elite but dependable. His ability to play both center and guard adds roster flexibility. Above-average pass protector, adequate in the run game.

trade_value.reasoning: Solid starting guard on a reasonable deal with 2 years left. Interior OL is a lower-value position in trade markets. His versatility (can play C or G) adds modest value. Worth a mid-to-late 4th round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: Brown is a quality starter but not irreplaceable. If Houston drafted or signed an upgrade at guard, he could be moved for fair value. He's not a core franchise piece — he's a solid veteran on a fair deal, the kind of player who changes teams for mid-round picks regularly.

players.name: Wyatt Teller
players.performance_evidence: Teller was a 2x Pro Bowler and 1st-team All-Pro (2021) with Cleveland, establishing himself as one of the NFL's premier run-blocking guards. PFF grades above 80 in multiple seasons with the Browns. Signed with Houston in free agency to bolster their interior line. In 2025 he remained a high-quality starter, though at 30 his dominance in the run game showed minor regression from his peak All-Pro seasons. Still graded as a top-15 guard by most advanced metrics.

trade_value.reasoning: Pro Bowl-caliber guard but age (31 in Sept 2026) and above-market AAV ($14M) limit his trade value. The age curve for guards drops off quickly in the early 30s. Still productive enough that a contending team would pay a 3rd-round pick equivalent, but the contract makes a swap less appealing.

players.availability_reasoning: Houston signed Teller to be a core piece of their offensive line rebuild. They won't give him away, but if a team offered a 3rd-round pick, they'd consider it — he's 31 and paid at the top of the guard market. Not being shopped, but not immovable at his age and price.
players.injury_notes: Has dealt with calf and bicep injuries at various points in his career. Missed 5 games in 2022 with a calf strain. Generally available but minor soft tissue concerns as he ages.

players.name: Calen Bullock
players.performance_evidence: Bullock stepped in as a rookie starter at free safety in 2024 and showed impressive playmaking ability — 3 interceptions, strong range in centerfield, and improving instincts against the run. PFF grades in the mid-60s as a rookie with significant upside based on his ball-hawking ability and coverage speed. At USC he was a 2-year starter with elite ball production (7 INTs in 2023). His trajectory suggests a potential Pro Bowl future as he gains NFL experience. Solid tackler for a free safety, though he can be inconsistent in run support.

trade_value.reasoning: 22-year-old starting FS on a cheap rookie deal with 3 years of control, including a 5th-year option if he develops as expected. The combination of youth, ascending play, and cost-controlled contract makes him worth an early-to-mid 2nd round pick despite safety being a lower-value position.

players.availability_reasoning: Bullock is a 22-year-old ascending starter on a rookie contract at a position of need across the NFL. Houston invested a 2nd-round pick in him and he delivered as a year-one starter. He's part of the long-term core alongside their other young defensive pieces. There is zero reason for Houston to trade him during their competitive window — he's cheap, young, and improving.
